📊 FinCalc Lab
Calculators Guides About

Financial answers in seconds

Free calculators for loans, savings, retirement and debt — with plain-English explanations of the math behind every result. No sign-up. Nothing tracked. Everything runs in your browser.

All calculators

Savings & Investing

📈 Compound Interest Calculator Project how savings grow with monthly deposits and compounding. 🎯 Savings Goal Calculator Find the exact monthly saving needed to hit a target amount. 🌴 Retirement Savings Calculator Project your nest egg and the income it could support. 📊 Investment Return (CAGR) Calculator Turn a total gain into an honest annualized return.

Loans & Debt

💳 Loan Payment Calculator Monthly payment, total interest and cost of any amortized loan. 🏠 Mortgage Calculator Full monthly housing cost: loan, property tax and insurance. 🚀 Loan Payoff Calculator See how extra monthly payments shorten a loan and cut interest. 🧊 Debt Avalanche vs Snowball Calculator Compare the two classic payoff strategies on your own debts.

Income & Budgeting

💰 Salary to Hourly Calculator Convert annual salary to hourly, weekly and monthly pay. 🧾 Take-Home Pay Estimator Estimate net monthly pay from gross salary and tax rates. 💸 50/30/20 Budget Calculator Split your take-home pay into needs, wants and savings. 🛟 Emergency Fund Calculator Size your emergency fund and see how long it takes to build. 🎈 Inflation Calculator See what today's money will be worth in the future.

Business

⚖️ Break-Even Point Calculator How many sales cover your costs — the first business question. 📉 Profit Margin Calculator Margin, markup and profit from cost and price.

Guides & explainers

"The 50/30/20 Rule in Real Life: Where It Works, Where It Breaks, How to Fix It" An honest field guide to the most famous budgeting rule — including what to do when rent alone eats 45% of your income. How Compound Interest Builds Wealth (and Why Starting Late Costs So Much) A plain-English walkthrough of compound growth, with real numbers showing why time in the market beats timing — and what starting five years late actually costs. "Emergency Fund vs Investing: Which Dollar Goes Where First?" A decision framework for the classic dilemma — cash safety versus market growth — including the order of operations most planners actually recommend. "How Much House Can You Afford? The 28/36 Rule and Its Honest Limits" A practical framework for setting your housing budget — the 28/36 rule, the costs first-time buyers forget, and when the guidelines mislead. "Marginal vs Effective Tax Rate: The Misunderstanding That Costs People Real Money" Why "being in the 24% bracket" doesn't mean losing 24% of your income, how progressive brackets actually work, and the raise myth debunked with numbers. "How to Pay Off Debt Faster: A Strategy Guide That Respects Both Math and Psychology" Avalanche, snowball, consolidation and balance transfers compared honestly — which debt payoff strategy fits your numbers and your temperament.

Why FinCalc Lab?

Private by design

Every calculation happens in your browser. Your salary, debts and savings never leave your device.

The math, explained

Each tool shows the formula it uses and walks through what the result actually means for you.

No paywalls, ever

Free to use, no accounts, no "premium" upsell. Supported by unobtrusive advertising.

FinCalc Lab

Free, fast and honest financial calculators. All tools run entirely in your browser — we never see or store your numbers.

About Contact Privacy Policy Terms of Use

© 2026 FinCalc Lab. All calculators and articles are for educational purposes only and do not constitute financial advice. Consult a qualified professional before making financial decisions.
